perturbation.original_text: Given the nature of the Confidential Information and our current discussions, the Company may be irreparably damaged by any unauthorized disclosure of any Confidential Information and you and the Company may be irreparably damaged by any unauthorized disclosure of our discussions or by any breach of this Agreement by you, the Company or the Representatives of you and the Company. Without prejudice to other rights and remedies otherwise available, you and the Company therefore agree that the other party hereto shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including an injunction or specific performance, in the event of any breach of the provisions of this Agreement by the other party hereto or the Representatives of the other party hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: Given the nature of the Confidential Information and our current discussions, the Company may be irreparably damaged by any unauthorized disclosure of any Confidential Information. Without prejudice to other rights and remedies otherwise available, you and the Company therefore agree that the other party hereto shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including an injunction or specific performance, only in the event of a direct breach of the provisions of this Agreement by the other party, but not the Representatives of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: Section 3 originally states that both parties can seek equitable relief for breaches of the agreement by the other party *or their Representatives*. The modified version removes the ability to seek relief for breaches caused by the Representatives, restricting it to direct breaches by the other party only. This directly contradicts the terms laid out previously in Section 1 where you agree you will be liable for any breaches of the terms of this Agreement by such Representatives. Now you're not. So, it contradicts itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You agree you will be liable for any breaches of the terms of this Agreement by such Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that equitable relief can be sought for breaches by the other party or their Representatives. The changed text limits this relief to only breaches by the other party directly, explicitly excluding breaches by the Representatives. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which states 'You agree you will be liable for any breaches of the terms of this Agreement by such Representatives.' Since the changed text removes the ability to seek equitable relief for actions the Contradicted Text makes you liable for, it is a direct and strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event you or any of your Representatives become legally compelled (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigation, demand, order or similar process) to disclose any of the contents of the Confidential Information, or you or the Company or any of the respective Representatives of you or the Company become so legally compelled to disclose either the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible Transaction between the Company and you, or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such possible Transaction, including the status thereof, the Company and you each agrees that each party and its respective Representatives may do so without liability, but each party hereto also agrees (i) to promptly notify the other party prior to any such disclosure to the extent practicable and legally permissible and (ii) to cooperate with the other party in any attempt it may make to obtain a protective order or other appropriate assurance that confidential treatment will be afforded the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event you become legally compelled (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigation, demand, order or similar process) to disclose any of the contents of the Confidential Information, the Company agrees that you may do so without liability, and you agree (i) to promptly notify the Company prior to any such disclosure to the extent practicable and legally permissible and (ii) to cooperate with the Company in any attempt it may make to obtain a protective order or other appropriate assurance that confidential treatment will be afforded the Confidential Information. This clause does not extend to your Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 4 covers scenarios where either the party or its Representatives are legally compelled to disclose Confidential Information. The modified version limits this protection to the party itself, explicitly stating that 'This clause does not extend to your Representatives.' This contradicts Section 1, where the definition of 'Representatives' is established, and the agreement holds you liable for their actions, including breaches. Therefore, if a Representative is compelled to disclose, the original agreement implied protection from liability, now explicitly removed. This creates uncertainty and potential legal conflicts.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You agree you will be liable for any breaches of the terms of this Agreement by such Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided liability protection to both the party and its representatives if legally compelled to disclose confidential information. The changed text removes this protection for the representatives, explicitly stating 'This clause does not extend to your Representatives.' This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states 'You agree you will be liable for any breaches of the terms of this Agreement by such Representatives.' If a representative is compelled to disclose and does so, the changed text removes liability protection that the original text afforded, thus creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Company may elect at any time to terminate further access by you to the Confidential Information. Following any written request by the Company, you agree, at the Company’s election, to either promptly redeliver to the Company or destroy all written Confidential Information in your possession or your Representatives’ possession, and you and your Representatives will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of such written material. You further agree that all documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by you or your Representatives based on the Confidential Information shall be destroyed, and you shall delete, to the extent reasonably practicable, all Confidential Information from any computer or other electronic device. Notwithstanding the foregoing, no such destruction or deletion shall be 2 required where prohibited by law, regulation, regulatory process or proceeding, or stock exchange regulation, or where the Confidential Information is part of the record of proceedings of your Board of Directors; provided , that any such Confidential Information so retained shall continue to be held confidential in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company may elect at any time to terminate further access by you to the Confidential Information. Following any written request by the Company, you agree to promptly redeliver to the Company or destroy all written Confidential Information in your possession, and you will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of such written material. You further agree that all documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by you based on the Confidential Information shall be destroyed, and you shall delete, to the extent reasonably practicable, all Confidential Information from any computer or other electronic device. There is no obligation to do so for the representatives.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 5 requires you and your Representatives to return or destroy Confidential Information upon the Company's request. The modified version removes the obligation for your Representatives to do so, stating 'There is no obligation to do so for the representatives'. This contradicts the underlying premise of the agreement that you are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of the information, especially in light of Section 1's declaration that you agree you will be liable for any breaches of the terms of this Agreement by such Representatives. Because, originally, the representative would have to comply, making you liable, but no, the representative doesn't comply, you should still be liable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You agree you will be liable for any breaches of the terms of this Agreement by such Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated that both the individual and their representatives were responsible for the handling of confidential information. The changed text explicitly removes the obligation for the representatives to adhere to this, which directly contradicts the section stating that the individual is liable for breaches by their representatives. If representatives are not obligated to maintain confidentiality, it creates a direct conflict and inconsistency with the liability clause, because the representative doesn't need to comply, and the individual is still liable. This aligns with the provided Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition. The change creates a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: You agree that certain Confidential Information may be deemed material non-public information under applicable securities laws. Without limitation of the other restrictions on use or transfer of Confidential Information described herein, you agree that while such information remains non-public, you will not engage, and will direct your Representatives not to engage, in any transactions in the securities of the Company in a manner which would constitute a violation of such laws.
perturbation.changed_text: You agree that certain Confidential Information may be deemed material non-public information under applicable securities laws. You agree that while such information remains non-public, you will not engage in any transactions in the securities of the Company in a manner which would constitute a violation of such laws. The company's representatives are not to be held liable for doing so.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 7 prohibits you and requires you to direct your Representatives not to engage in illegal securities transactions using Confidential Information. The modified version removes the requirement to direct your Representatives, explicitly stating that Representatives are not to be held liable, removing restrictions from the representative. This contradicts Section 1, where the definition of 'Representatives' is established, and the agreement holds you liable for their actions, including breaches.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You agree you will be liable for any breaches of the terms of this Agreement by such Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for the user to direct their representatives to not engage in illegal securities transactions, and explicitly states that representatives are not to be held liable. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states 'You agree you will be liable for any breaches of the terms of this Agreement by such Representatives'. The change removes liability from representatives while the contradicted section enforces liability upon them, therefore causing a strong in-text contradiction as per the provided Structural Flaws definition.
